description: Use this API to insert a bus at a designated location along a line. This API routine was first introduced in release 28.0.0. ierr = ltap(frmbus, tobus, ckt, fraction, newnum, newnam, newkv)
python_syntax: ierr = ltap(frmbus, tobus, ckt, fraction, newnum, newnam, newkv)

parameters.name: frmbus
parameters.description: One of the buses connected by the branch to be tapped. (input; no default allowed).

parameters.name: tobus
parameters.description: Other bus connected by the branch to be tapped. (input; no default allowed).

parameters.name: ckt
parameters.description: Circuit identifier of the branch to be tapped. (input; ‘1’ by default).

parameters.name: fraction
parameters.description: New bus location relative to bus frmbus in per unit of total line length. (input; 0.5 by default).

parameters.name: newnum
parameters.description: Number to be assigned to the new bus. (input; no default allowed).

parameters.name: newnam
parameters.description: Name to be assigned to the new bus. (input; blank by default).

parameters.name: newkv
parameters.description: Base voltage to be assigned to the new bus. (input; base voltage of bus FRMBUS by default).

error_codes.description: bus number is already assigned to another bus.
bus, branch, or branch ownership tables full.
prerequisite requirements for API are not met.
